frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with control panel prompt visible.
The needle is loaded onto the needle driver.
The needle is being placed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears accurate with slight deviation noted.

frame_analyses.description: Forming the first throw of the knot using a double throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: A surgeon's knot is achieved using the right hand.

frame_analyses.description: The first single throw is being tied.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switching hands to left for the second throw.

frame_analyses.description: The second single throw is being applied with a hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switching hands to right for consistency.

frame_analyses.description: Preparing to cut the suture ends.
Both suture ends have been cut and task appears complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The drain remains in place, no slippage of knot detected.

technique_summary: The task was performed efficiently with minimal deviation from ideal suture placement. The knot was secure and the drain remained intact. Overall, the procedure was well-executed.
improvement_suggestions: Maintain a consistent pace throughout the task
